October 12, 1978 
Mr. Rich Eichmann 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Dear Mr. Eichmann: 
Recently we called and spoke with you for clarification 
on our lobbying report. 
Your assistance is greatly 
appreciated. 
However, a recent audit of our reports by 
Mike Rand elicited the comment that we should get special 
rulings in writing. 
I'd like to re-state the question 
that we have, and ask for your written ruling. 
Kaiser Aluminum & Chemical Corporation, as employer of a 
lobbyist, is continuing to make political contributions 
even though there is currently no registered lobbyist in 
the company_ 
The registration of Timothy J. Conlon was 
terminated effective September 30, 1978. 
How should these contributions be reported for the months 
of October, November, and December? 
We do not expect to 
have a registered lobbyist until December, when we will 
likely be registering for the new session. 
Thanks for your help. 
Ish 
Richard L. 
pees 
Vice President 
Western Region Public 
fairs

sections.question: Kaiser Aluminum & Chemical Corporation, as employer of a lobbyist, is continuing to make political contributions even though there is currently no registered lobbyist in the company. The registration of Timothy J. Conlon was terminated effective September 30, 1978. How should these contributions be reported for the months of October, November, and December? We do not expect to have a registered lobbyist until December, when we will likely be registering for the new session.
sections.conclusion: If Kaiser does not employ a lobbyist during October or November reports need not be filed pursuant to Government Code Section 86108(a). However, if Kaiser directly or indirectly makes payments to influence legislative or administrative action of $250 or more in October or November reports will be required pursuant to Government Code Section 86108(b). If Kaiser is required to file a report for October or November pursuant to Government Code Section 86108(b) all contributions to state and local candidates and measures must be disclosed on the report. If a lobbyist registers on behalf of Kaiser in December or Kaiser spends $250 or more in December to influence legislative or administrative action a report must be filed pursuant to Government Code Section 86109 which would disclose all of the activities during the month of December including contributions made to state and local candidates and measures.

sections.question_synthetic: Kaiser asked how to report political contributions made in October, November, and December when they do not currently employ a registered lobbyist (lobbyist registration terminated September 30, 1978) but expect to have one again in December.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Kaiser must file reports in October or November only if they make payments of $250 or more to influence legislative or administrative action; if a registered lobbyist is obtained in December or $250+ is spent that month on lobbying activities, a December report disclosing all contributions is required. The FPPC provides the statutory definition of reportable 'payments to influence legislative or administrative action' in five categories.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with some text degradation and redaction marks. Question section clearly identified in incoming request letter (reversed chronologically in document). Conclusion section in FPPC response letter clearly delineated. Some month names appear redacted or unclear but principal advice is clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Kaiser Aluminum & Chemical Corporation, as employer of a lobbyist, is continuing to make political contributions even though there is currently no registered lobbyist in the company. The registration of Timothy J. Conlon was terminated effective September 30, 1978. How should these contributions be reported for the months of October, November, and December? We do not expect to have a registered lobbyist until December, when we will likely be registering for the new session.

CONCLUSION: If Kaiser does not employ a lobbyist during October or November reports need not be filed pursuant to Government Code Section 86108(a). However, if Kaiser directly or indirectly makes payments to influence legislative or administrative action of $250 or more in October or November reports will be required pursuant to Government Code Section 86108(b). If Kaiser is required to file a report for October or November pursuant to Government Code Section 86108(b) all contributions to state and local candidates and measures must be disclosed on the report. If a lobbyist registers on behalf of Kaiser in December or Kaiser spends $250 or more in December to influence legislative or administrative action a report must be filed pursuant to Government Code Section 86109 which would disclose all of the activities during the month of December including contributions made to state and local candidates and measures.

embedding.summary: Kaiser Aluminum requested written guidance on reporting political contributions during a period when they had no registered lobbyist (September 30-December 1978). The FPPC advised that reports are only required in October-November if $250+ is spent to influence legislative or administrative action, and that December reporting requirements depend on whether a lobbyist is registered or equivalent spending occurs that month.
